title: Carve-out for employee stock options
text: outstanding options granted to employees, whether currently an employee or not, pursuant to an employee stock option scheme in compliance with the Companies Act, 2013, the relevant Guidance Note or accounting standards, if any, issued by the Institute of Chartered Accountants of India or pursuant to the Companies Act, 2013, in this regard;
notes: Carve-out for employee stock options; no conditions specified beyond scheme compliance.

source.span_hint: outstanding options granted to employees

title: Carve-out for stock appreciation rights
text: 27[(b) outstanding stock appreciation rights granted to employees pursuant to a stock appreciation right scheme, which are fully exercised for equity shares prior to the filing of the red herring prospectus (in case of book-built issues) or the prospectus (in case of fixed price issues), as the case may be, disclosures regarding such stock appreciation rights and the scheme and the total number of equity shares resulting from the exercise of such rights are made in the draft offer document and offer document.]
notes: Carve-out for stock appreciation rights; requires pre-filing exercise and disclosures.

source.span_hint: outstanding stock appreciation rights

title: Carve-out for fully paid convertible securities
text: 28[(c)] fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities which are required to be converted on or before the date of filing of the red herring prospectus (in case of book-built issues) or the prospectus (in case of fixed price issues), as the case may be.
notes: Carve-out for fully paid mandatorily convertible securities; conversion must be completed by filing date.

source.span_hint: fully paid-up outstanding convertible securities

title: Net tangible assets requirement
text: it has net tangible assets of at least three crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), of which not more than fifty per cent. are held in monetary assets: Provided that if more than fifty per cent. of the net tangible assets are held in monetary assets, the issuer has utilised or made firm commitments to utilise such excess monetary assets in its business or project; Provided further that the limit of fifty per cent. on monetary assets shall not be applicable in case the initial public offer is made entirely through an offer for sale.
notes: Map to net_tangible_assets >= 3cr in each of last 3 years; includes conditions on monetary assets.

source.span_hint: net tangible assets

title: Average operating profit requirement
text: it has an average operating profit of at least fifteen crore rupees, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, during the preceding three years (of twelve months each), with operating profit in each of these preceding three years;
notes: Map to operating_profit average >=15cr in last 3 years; each year's profit must be at least 15cr.

source.span_hint: average operating profit

title: Net worth requirement
text: it has a net worth of at least one crore rupees in each of the preceding three full years (of twelve months each), calculated on a restated and consolidated basis;
notes: Map to net_worth >=1cr in each of last 3 years.

source.span_hint: net worth

title: Name change revenue source requirement
text: if it has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year has been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.
notes: Check for name change within last year and revenue source alignment.

source.span_hint: if it has changed its name

title: Eligibility for book-building process
text: An issuer not satisfying the condition stipulated in sub-regulation (1) shall be eligible to make an initial public offer only if the issue is made through the book-building process and the issuer undertakes to allot at least seventy five per cent. of the net offer to qualified institutional buyers and to refund the full subscription money if it fails to do so.
notes: Eligibility for book-building with minimum QIB allotment and refund condition.

source.span_hint: An issuer not satisfying

title: Special conditions for SR equity shares
text: 29[(3) If an issuer has issued SR equity shares to its promoters/ founders, the said issuer shall be allowed to do an initial public offer of only ordinary shares for listing on the Main Board subject to compliance with the provisions of this Chapter and these clauses -
notes: Special conditions for SR equity shares; requires compliance with Chapter and clauses.

source.span_hint: If an issuer has issued SR equity shares

title: Industry focus requirement for SR shares
text: i. the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology, information technology, intellectual property, data analytics, bio-technology or nano-technology to provide products, services or business platforms with substantial value addition.
notes: Requires issuer to be in specified high-tech industries.

source.span_hint: intensive in the use of technology

title: Net worth limit for SR shareholder
text: ii. the net worth of the SR shareholder, as determined by a Registered Valuer, shall not be more than rupees one thousand crore.
notes: Net worth of SR shareholder capped at ₹1000 crore.

source.span_hint: net worth of the SR shareholder

title: Authorization of SR equity shares
text: iv. The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized by a special resolution passed at a general meeting of the shareholders of the issuer, where the notice calling for such general meeting specifically provided for - a. the size of issue of SR equity shares, b. ratio of voting rights of SR equity shares vis-à-vis the ordinary shares,
notes: Requires special resolution with specified details in the notice.

source.span_hint: The issue of SR equity shares had been authorized

title: Name change with 50% revenue from new activity
text: if it has changed its name within the last one year, at least fifty per cent. of the revenue, calculated on a restated and consolidated basis, for the preceding one full year has been earned by it from the activity indicated by its new name.
notes: Map to has_name_change with revenue condition ≥50% from new activity.

source.span_hint: if it has changed its name

title: Condition for SR equity shares issuance
text: If an issuer has issued SR equity shares to its promoters/ founders, the said issuer shall be allowed to do an initial public offer of only ordinary shares for listing on the Main Board subject to compliance with the provisions of this Chapter and these clauses -
notes: Issuer with SR equity shares can only offer ordinary shares if conditions are met.

source.span_hint: If an issuer has issued SR equity shares

title: Intensive use of technology and innovation
text: the issuer shall be intensive in the use of technology, information technology, intellectual property, data analytics, bio-technology or nano-technology to provide products, services or business platforms with substantial value addition.
notes: Map to uses_technology_indicators for substantial value addition.

source.span_hint: intensive in the use of technology

title: Same face value for SR equity shares
text: The SR equity shares shall have the same face value as the ordinary shares;
notes: Map to same_face_value as ordinary shares.

source.span_hint: same face value

title: Depository agreement for dematerialisation
text: it has entered into an agreement with a depository for dematerialisation of the specified securities already issued and proposed to be issued;
notes: Map to has_depository_agreement.

source.span_hint: entered into an agreement with a depository

title: Dematerialised promoters' shares
text: all its specified securities held by the promoters are in dematerialised form prior to filing of the offer document;
notes: Map to promoters_shares_dematerialised.

source.span_hint: all its specified securities held by the promoters

title: Partly paid-up shares fully paid or forfeited
text: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares have either been fully paid-up or have been forfeited;
notes: Map to partly_paid_up_shares fully_paid or forfeited.

source.span_hint: all its existing partly paid-up equity shares

title: Finance arrangements for 75%
text: it has made firm arrangements of finance through verifiable means towards seventy five per cent. of the stated means of finance for a specific project proposed to be funded from the issue proceeds, excluding the amount to be raised through the proposed public issue or through existing identifiable internal accruals.
notes: Map to has_finance_arrangements for 75%.

source.span_hint: made firm arrangements of finance

title: GCP amount not exceeding 25%
text: The amount for general corporate purposes, as mentioned in objects of the issue in the draft offer document and the offer document shall not exceed twenty five per cent. of the amount being raised by the issuer.
notes: Map to general_corporate_purpose_amount <= 25%.

source.span_hint: The amount for general corporate purposes

title: Shareholding limit for large shareholders
text: shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding based on fully diluted basis, shall not exceed more than fifty per cent of their pre-issue shareholding on fully diluted basis;
notes: Map to max_shareholding_percentage for shareholders with >20% pre-issue.

source.span_hint: shares offered for sale

title: Shareholding limit for small shareholders
text: shares offered for sale to the public by shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, less than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding based on fully diluted basis, shall not exceed more than ten per cent of pre-issue shareholding of the issuer on fully diluted basis;
notes: Map to max_shareholding_percentage for shareholders with <20% pre-issue.

source.span_hint: shares offered for sale

title: Lock-in provisions for large shareholders
text: for shareholder(s) holding, individually or with persons acting in concert, more than twenty per cent of pre-issue shareholding based on fully diluted basis, provisions of lock-in as specified under regulation 17 of these regulations shall be applicable, and relaxation from lock-in as provided under clause (c) of regulation 17 of these regulations shall not be applicable.
notes: Map to lock-in requirements from regulation 17.

source.span_hint: provisions of lock-in

title: Security cover after reducing first charge
text: the security or asset cover shall be arrived at after reduction of the liabilities having a first or prior charge, in case the convertible debt instruments are secured by a second or subsequent charge.
notes: Map to security_cover calculation after reducing first charge.

source.span_hint: security or asset cover shall be arrived at

title: Redemption of convertible debt in terms of offer document
text: The issuer shall redeem the convertible debt instruments in terms of the offer document.
notes: Map to redemption requirements based on offer document.

source.span_hint: shall redeem

title: No conversion without consent
text: The issuer shall not convert its optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares unless the holders of such convertible debt instruments have sent their positive consent to the issuer and non-receipt of reply to any notice sent by the issuer for this purpose shall not be construed as consent for conversion of any convertible debt instruments.
notes: Map to consent requirement for conversion.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: shall not convert

title: Option to not convert if price not determined
text: Where the value of the convertible portion of any listed convertible debt instruments issued by an issuer exceeds ten crore rupees and the issuer has not determined the conversion price of such convertible debt instruments at the time of making the issue, the holders of such convertible debt instruments shall be given the option of not converting the convertible portion into equity shares: Provided that where the upper limit on the price of such convertible debt instruments and justification thereon is determined and disclosed to the investors at the time of making the issue, it shall not be necessary to give such option to the holders of the convertible debt instruments for converting the convertible portion into equity share capital within the said upper limit.
notes: Map to conversion price disclosure and option for non-conversion.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: Where the value

title: Redemption if option not exercised
text: Where an option is to be given to the holders of the convertible debt instruments in terms of sub-regulation (2) and if one or more of such holders do not exercise the option to convert the instruments into equity share capital at a price determined in the general meeting of the shareholders, the issuer shall redeem that part of the instruments within one month from the last date by which option is to be exercised, at a price which shall not be less than its face value.
notes: Map to redemption timeline and minimum price.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: Where an option

title: Exception for redemption as per disclosures
text: The provision of sub-regulation (2) shall not apply if such redemption is as per the disclosures made in the offer document.
notes: Map to disclosure requirements for exemption.

source.reg: Conversion of optionally convertible debt instruments into equity shares
source.span_hint: The provision

title: Restriction on issuing convertible debt to promoter-related parties
text: An issuer shall not issue convertible debt instruments for financing or for providing loans to or for acquiring shares of any person who is part of the promoter group or group companies: Provided that an issuer shall be eligible to issue fully convertible debt instruments for these purposes if the period of conversion of such debt instruments is less than eighteen months from the date of issue of such debt instruments.
notes: Map to promoter group restrictions and conversion period.

source.reg: Issue of convertible debt instruments for financing
source.span_hint: An issuer shall not

title: Warrant tenure ≤ 18 months
text: the tenure of such warrants shall not exceed eighteen months from the date of their allotment in the initial public offer;
notes: Map to warrant expiry date.

source.span_hint: tenure of such warrants

title: Multiple warrants per security
text: a specified security may have one or more warrants attached to it;
notes: Map to maximum warrants per security.

source.span_hint: a specified security may have

title: Upfront determination and receipt for warrant exercise
text: the price or formula for determination of exercise price of the warrants shall be determined upfront and disclosed in the offer document and at least twenty-five per cent. of the consideration amount based on the exercise price shall also be received upfront; Provided that in case the exercise price of warrants is based on a formula, twenty-five per cent. consideration amount based on the cap price of the price band determined for the linked equity shares or convertible securities shall be received upfront.
notes: Map to exercise price determination, upfront receipt, and formula handling.

source.span_hint: the price or formula

title: Forfeiture if warrant not exercised
text: in case the warrant holder does not exercise the option to take equity shares against any of the warrants held by the warrant holder, within three months from the date of payment of consideration, such consideration made in respect of such warrants shall be forfeited by the issuer.
notes: Map to forfeiture conditions and timeline.

source.span_hint: in case the warrant holder

title: Minimum promoters' contribution ≥ 20%
text: The promoters of the issuer shall hold at least twenty per cent. of the post-issue capital: Provided that in case the post-issue shareholding of the promoters is less than twenty per cent., alternative investment funds or foreign venture capital investors or scheduled commercial banks or public financial institutions or insurance companies registered with Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India or any non-individual public shareholder holding at least five per cent. of the post-issue capital or any entity (individual or non-individual) forming part of promoter group other than the promoter(s) may contribute to meet the shortfall in minimum contribution as specified for the promoters, subject to a maximum of ten per cent. of the post-issue capital without being identified as promoter(s). Provided further that the requirement of minimum promoters’ contribution shall not apply in case an issuer does not have any identifiable promoter.
notes: Map to promoters' minimum shareholding or alternative contributions.

source.reg: PART III: PROMOTERS’ CONTRIBUTION Minimum promoters’ contribution
source.span_hint: The promoters of the issuer shall hold

title: Promoters' contribution method
text: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent. as stipulated in sub-regulation (1), as the case may be, either by way of equity shares or by way of subscription to convertible securities: Provided that if the price of the equity shares allotted pursuant to conversion is not pre-determined and not disclosed in the offer document, the promoters shall contribute only by way of subscription to the convertible securities being issued in the public issue and shall undertake in writing to subscribe to the equity shares pursuant to conversion of such securities.
notes: Map to contribution_method: equity or convertible, with condition for price not pre-determined.

source.span_hint: the promoters shall contribute twenty per cent

title: Promoters' contribution price
text: in case of any issue of convertible securities which are convertible or exchangeable on different dates and if the promoters’ contribution is by way of equity shares (conversion price being pre-determined), such contribution shall not be at a price lower than the weighted average price of the equity share capital arising out of conversion of such securities.
notes: Map to contribution_price not lower than weighted average.

source.span_hint: in case of any issue of convertible securities

title: Promoters' contribution in debt instruments
text: subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above, in case of an initial public offer of convertible debt instruments without a prior public issue of equity shares, the promoters shall bring in a contribution of at least twenty per cent. of the project cost in the form of equity shares, subject to contributing at least twenty per cent. of the issue size from their own funds in the form of equity shares: Provided that if the project is to be implemented in stages, the promoters’ contribution shall be with respect to total equity participation till the respective stage vis-à-vis the debt raised or proposed to be raised through the public issue.
notes: Map to contribution_min (20% project cost or issue size).

source.span_hint: subject to the provisions of clause (a) and (b) above

title: Deadline for promoters' contribution
text: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements of this regulation at least one day prior to the date of opening of the issue.
notes: Map to satisfy_requirements by specific date.

source.span_hint: The promoters shall satisfy the requirements

title: Escrow for promoters' contribution
text: In case the promoters have to subscribe to equity shares or convertible securities towards minimum promoters’ contribution, the amount of promoters’ contribution shall be kept in an escrow account with a scheduled commercial bank, which shall be released to the issuer along with the release of the issue proceeds: Provided that where the promoters’ contribution has already been brought in and utilised, the issuer shall give the cash flow statement disclosing the use of such funds in the offer document; Provided further that where the minimum promoters’ contribution is more than one hundred crore rupees and the initial public offer is for partly paid shares, the promoters shall bring in at least one hundred crore rupees before the date of opening of the issue and the remaining amount may be brought on a pro-rata basis before the calls are made to the public.
notes: Map to escrow_account and conditions for release.

source.span_hint: In case the promoters have to subscribe

title: Promoter contribution: acquisition under approved scheme
text: Specified securities acquired by promoters under schemes approved by High Court, tribunal or Central Government under sections 230-234 of Companies Act, 2013 must be held for at least one year prior to filing.
notes: Map to promoter_contribution with lock_in_period ≥ 1 year for securities acquired under approved schemes.

source.span_hint: acquired in terms of the scheme

title: Promoter contribution: infrastructure sector exemption
text: Government companies, statutory authorities, corporations or SPVs engaged in infrastructure sector may receive specified securities without meeting promoter contribution requirements.
notes: Map to is_eligible for infrastructure entities.

source.span_hint: to an initial public offer by a government company

title: Calculation of ineligible securities
text: Price per share for determining ineligible securities shall be adjusted for corporate actions like share splits, bonus issues etc.
notes: Map to ineligible_securities calculation with corporate_action adjustment.

source.span_hint: price per share for determining securities ineligible

title: Promoter contribution: pledged securities
text: Specified securities pledged with any creditor shall not be considered as promoter contribution.
notes: Map to is_eligible for pledged securities.

source.span_hint: specified securities pledged with any creditor

title: Lock-in period for pre-issue capital
text: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in for a period of six months from the date of allotment in the initial public offer.
notes: Map to lock_in_period for non_promoter_holders (6 months).

source.span_hint: The entire pre-issue capital held by persons other than the promoters shall be locked-in

title: Carve-out: pre-IPO employee stock options
text: Equity shares allotted to employees under an employee stock option or employee stock purchase scheme or a stock appreciation right scheme prior to the initial public offer shall be subject to lock-in, unless the issuer has made full disclosures in accordance with Part A of Schedule VI.
notes: Map to is_employees_stock_options + has_disclosed (Part A Schedule VI).

source.span_hint: equity shares allotted to employees, whether currently an employee or not

title: Carve-out: employee stock option trust
text: Equity shares held by an employee stock option trust or transferred to employees by the trust shall be subject to lock-in provisions of the Securities and Exchange Board of India (Share Based Employee Benefits and Sweat Equity) Regulations, 2021.
notes: Map to is_employee_stock_option_trust + must_follow_SEBI_2021_regulations.

source.span_hint: equity shares held by an employee stock option trust

title: Carve-out: venture capital funds
text: Equity shares held by a venture capital fund or alternative investment fund of category I or Category II or a foreign venture capital investor shall be locked-in for a period of at least six months from the date of purchase.
notes: Map to is_venture_capital_fund + lock_in_period (6 months from purchase).

source.span_hint: equity shares held by a venture capital fund

title: Lock-in exemption for stabilizing agent
text: The lock-in provisions shall not apply to specified securities lent to a stabilizing agent for the purpose of green shoe option during the period from the date of allotment until the end of the initial public offer.
notes: Map to lock_in_exempted_for_stabilizing_agent.

source.span_hint: The lock-in provisions shall not apply
